text.div: Coronary artery disease confirmed on imaging, management plan established

Dear Colleague, I am writing to inform you regarding your patient, a 56-year-old male admitted on 07/04/2023 for routine post-coronary artery bypass grafting follow-up. On admission, a thorough cardiac assessment was performed, including electrocardiography, echocardiography, and laboratory investigations. Cardiac biomarkers were within normal limits, and haemodynamic parameters remained stable throughout his stay. Graft patency was considered satisfactory based on clinical and non-invasive findings. No signs of post-operative complications, including wound infection, arrhythmia, or heart failure, were identified. Medication reconciliation was performed, and current cardioprotective therapy was reviewed and optimised. The patient was counselled regarding lifestyle modifications, cardiac rehabilitation attendance, and warning signs requiring urgent review. He was discharged on 08/04/2023 in stable condition. Please continue routine follow-up. Should you have any concerns, do not hesitate to contact us.
